sections: HCM-0.4 crate ownership and dependency contract

repo_truth: The exact three-file HCM-0.5 planning manifest is the complete review subject. Canonical 00-06 files remain unchanged and preliminary for HCM-0.5. Historical Planning Reviews 1-4 and their dispatches are immutable evidence. This invocation is terminal Continuation Planning Review 2; no further planning remediation or review is authorized. A later fresh canonical design-freeze session reserves Design Reviews 1-3 and two remediations. The exact dependency graph is: selected exhausted-budget handoff -> explicit human continuation -> R4-001/R4-002 planning repair -> Continuation Planning Review 1 -> at most one bounded planning remediation -> Continuation Planning Review 2. CLEAN -> complete packet validation and GitNexus detection -> approved planning commit -> validated partial/context-boundary handoff binding that commit and exact packet -> fresh session selecting that exact handoff -> Tasks 3-5 -> complete proof wall -> Design Review 1. Any non-clean Continuation Planning Review 2 -> Task 7 byte-identical handoff and stop. Tasks 3-6 and 8-10 remain prohibited until Task 2B, including its handoff, is complete; Task 7 remains executable solely for a terminal non-completion handoff. Future design findings permit at most two remediations and Design Reviews 2-3; terminal non-clean transitions byte-identically to Task 7 and no Design Review 4.
allowed_scope: Read and replay the exact three-file subject manifest and the named authority sections.
Run read-only Markdown, schema/example, link, portability, Git, review-lineage, and handoff checks needed to validate the plan.

Review the complete planning subject for internal consistency, exact dependency ordering, stage-scoped review identities, byte-identical terminal handoff behavior, complete final-design subject timing, and independently executable future design-review budget.
non_goals: No repository edits, commits, new dispatches, global handoff, ledger write, or user task hop by the reviewer.

No canonical 00-06 design edits, 05 contract changes, Rust, Cargo, runtime, CLI, Tauri, SDK, Substrate, dock implementation, HCM-0.6 activation, HCM-0.9 change, catalog leaf, or substantive semantic redesign.

Do not treat a planning review as final-design review, rename a continuation review Review 5, schedule Design Review 4, rewrite historical dispatches/handoffs, or authorize canonical design work from a non-clean planning subject.
tasks: Replay the complete three-file subject manifest and aggregate fingerprint before reviewing content.

Verify HCM-0.5-R4-001 is repaired: no terminal review permits a local subject correction; every non-clean terminal review transitions to a non-completion handoff with the identical manifest/fingerprint and no intervening subject-byte change.

Verify HCM-0.5-R4-002 is repaired: Tasks 3-6 and 8-10 are prohibited until Task 2B is complete, including the validated partial/context-boundary handoff and fresh-session selection of that exact handoff, while Task 7 remains executable for a terminal non-completion handoff.

Verify the exact dependency graph in repo_truth is represented consistently in SPEC.md, plan.md, and todo.md without an unreachable handoff, missing resume-selector gate, or premature canonical-design branch.

Verify historical Planning Reviews 1-4 remain unchanged; Continuation Planning Reviews 1-2 are distinct and exhausted by this invocation; Design Reviews 1-3 are reserved for the fresh future session and permit at most two remediations; every stage stops immediately on CLEAN; no continuation Review 5 or Design Review 4 exists.

Verify final intended 00-README.md status bytes are authored before the complete proof wall, included in every Design Review manifest, made effective only by the clean reviewed design commit, and never changed after CLEAN.

Review whether the future execution plan still fully closes the HCM-0.5 semantic questions, owner boundaries, process-dock posture, HCM-0.4 regression boundary, proof-before-review ordering, complete final-design manifest, and two-commit closeout without adding implementation authority.
Review non-goals, current-session scope, exact clean and non-clean closeout paths, and the rule that planning review never satisfies final-design review.

Return findings first ordered Critical, Required, Optional, Nit with exact file/line, violated authority/objective, impact, smallest remediation, and missing verification. Return CLEAN only if no actionable planning finding remains.
contracts_and_gates: Authorized terminal finding HCM-0.5-R4-001: terminal non-clean subject bytes must be preserved unchanged into the non-completion handoff.

Authorized terminal finding HCM-0.5-R4-002: the pre-planning-clean task prohibition must exclude Task 7 while continuing to block canonical design, proof, completion, and implementation tasks until the validated planning handoff is selected by a fresh session.
Historical stage: immutable Planning Reviews 1-4 and Remediations 1-3; no renumbering or rewrite.
Continuation stage: this is terminal Continuation Planning Review 2; stop immediately on CLEAN; any non-clean result transitions byte-identically to Task 7 with no further remediation.
Future design stage: maximum three complete final-design reviews, maximum two remediations, stop immediately on CLEAN, no Design Review 4, and honest byte-identical handoff on terminal non-clean.

Verification evidence for the current subject: planning phrase/state/dependency assertions passed; changed paths are exactly the three packet files plus additive continuation dispatch evidence; all four historical dispatches match HEAD; archive boundary and negative self-test passed; all three handoff validator modes passed; git diff --check passed.
stop_conditions: Return a Required finding if the planning packet contains any actionable defect, including an inconsistent task edge, manifest boundary, or review-stage transition.
Return blocked if the exact subject/authority or required read-only capability is unavailable.
Do not modify repository state or write orchestration artifacts.
Do not authorize canonical design edits, implementation, HCM-0.6, HCM-0.9, Review 5, or Design Review 4.
